primary_accounting_issue: Éligibilité du risque de change d’une créance de dividendes intragroupe comme élément couvert dans une relation de couverture au niveau des états financiers consolidés

authority_resolution.selection_reason: IAS 39 is selected because the question is about hedge-accounting eligibility and designation of a hedged item in consolidated financial statements, and IAS 21 expressly excludes hedge accounting while IAS 39 directly governs hedged items and hedge accounting models; no higher-precedence overlapping IFRS standard is provided in the context.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: low — the core issue is hedge-accounting eligibility, and IAS 39 directly addresses that issue whereas IAS 21 only provides supporting foreign-currency and consolidation context.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern eligible hedged items in consolidated financial statements and the named hedge accounting models.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs clarify that hedge accounting is outside IAS 21, define monetary-item FX translation, and explain when intragroup monetary FX differences survive consolidation.
This interpretation clarifies the distinct net investment hedge model and the foreign-currency risk that may be designated in consolidated financial statements.

peripheral_authority.reason: Related-party disclosure requirements are not relevant to identifying hedge-accounting approach families.
Insurance-contract foreign currency measurement is unrelated to hedge-accounting eligibility for intragroup dividends receivable.
Financial instrument presentation and offsetting do not identify the peer hedge-accounting models for this issue.
Income tax consequences do not govern the hedge-accounting model selection for the FX risk in question.
Provision guidance is not relevant to identifying hedge-accounting approaches for FX risk on an intragroup receivable.
Cash flow statement disclosures do not affect the hedge-accounting model framework.
Business-combination accounting for insurance contracts is unrelated to this hedge-accounting issue.
Government grant accounting is unrelated to the identification of hedge-accounting models here.
Employee benefit measurement is not relevant to hedge-accounting approach identification.
Hyperinflation translation requirements are not relevant to the hedge-accounting models at issue.
Retirement benefit plan accounting is unrelated to this issue.
Disclosure simplifications do not govern hedge-accounting eligibility or model selection.
Non-cash dividend distribution accounting is not relevant to hedge-accounting models for FX risk on intragroup dividends receivable.
Member-share classification is unrelated to this hedge-accounting issue.
Tax-status changes are not relevant to hedge-accounting approach identification.

treatment_families.family: IAS 39 hedge accounting model families for eligible foreign-currency exposures
Eligibility of intragroup items in consolidated financial statements as hedged items

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 39 identifies fair value hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model and allows foreign currency risk of a firm commitment to be accounted for as such.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 39 identifies cash flow hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model and specifically addresses highly probable forecast transactions, including certain intragroup foreign-currency transactions, in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 39 and IFRIC 16 identify the hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct top-level hedge accounting model for foreign-currency exposure in consolidated financial statements.
